inventory_days_of_cover_support: Yes, but only as a country-specific seasonal proxy; no public global daily stock ledger found.

key_exposure_or_disruption_figures: 34% of global urea trade moves via Hormuz; 800 kt on 17 ships blocked west of Hormuz at end-April; only 2 urea vessels carrying about 85 kt exited between late Feb and end-Apr.

inventory_days_of_cover_support: Partial at best; U.S. public data exist only at aggregate nitrogen-nutrient level, not a clean ammonia stock ledger.
public_source: USDA AgTransport annual nitrogen inventory/disappearance; no comparable public ammonia inventory series found for Hormuz-exposed exporters/importers.
likely_blog_treatment: Mostly qualitative mechanism story, with any quantitative case limited to a jurisdiction-specific proxy.
key_exposure_or_disruption_figures: 23% of global ammonia trade moves via Hormuz; 60 kt on 3 ships blocked west of Hormuz at end-April; Iran halted ammonia production, Qatar suspended ammonia output, and India cut ammonia output because of lower LNG supplies.
confidence: Medium on exposure, low on buffer.
notes: Use as feedstock/exposure row, not a hard inventory-buffer claim.

inventory_days_of_cover_support: No reliable public inventory series found for a credible days-of-cover claim.
public_source: Exposure and blocked-shipment evidence are public; true stock data are not.
likely_blog_treatment: Qualitative only, unless a proprietary terminal/tank dataset appears.
key_exposure_or_disruption_figures: About 49% of global seaborne sulfur trade moves via Hormuz; about 400 kt on 10 ships remained blocked west of Hormuz at end-April; only 4 sulfur vessels carrying about 200 kt exited between late Feb and end-Apr.
confidence: High on exposure, low on buffer.
notes: Sulfur is a strong disruption story, but not a good inventory-buffer story.

public_source: India parliamentary opening/closing stock answers for DAP; USDA AgTransport dashboard for U.S. phosphorus nutrient inventory/disappearance.
likely_blog_treatment: Quantitative India case study plus global qualitative framing.
key_exposure_or_disruption_figures: 18% of global MAP+DAP trade moves via Hormuz; 150 kt of phosphate fertilizers on 3 ships blocked west of Hormuz at end-April; India closing stock was 21.61 LMT on 5 Mar 2026 and 13.11 LMT on 15 Jul 2025; DAP imports from China fell from 22.28 LMT in 2023-24 to 8.47 LMT in 2024-25.
confidence: Medium-high on exposure, medium on buffer.
notes: Good candidate for a country case study; not enough for a clean global stock series.

inventory_days_of_cover_support: Yes, but it looks like a control case rather than a Hormuz-sensitive buffer story.
public_source: India parliamentary stock answers for MOP; USDA AgTransport dashboard for U.S. potash inventory/disappearance.

public_source: Public sources cover feedstock exposure and price effects, not inventories.
likely_blog_treatment: Qualitative only unless a proprietary stock dataset becomes available.
key_exposure_or_disruption_figures: IEA says the steepest losses have occurred in the petrochemical sector because feedstock availability is constrained; 30% of all LPG exported by sea transited Hormuz in 2025; the Strait carries over 110 bcm of LNG with no alternative routes; World Bank says the crisis affects petrochemicals, chemicals, and heavy manufacturing.
notes: Good mechanism story, weak inventory story.
